title: Supervised Learning: Learning with an Answer Key
concept: Supervised learning trains a model on labeled examples — pairs of input and correct answer — checking every guess against the answer key until it learns to guess right.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Which type of machine learning learns by taking actions and getting rewards or penalties?
exercises.explain: Right! Reinforcement learning is driven by rewards and penalties from taking actions.

cards.title: The Practice Test with Answers in the Back
cards.body: Imagine studying with a workbook where every practice question has the correct answer printed in the back. You guess, flip to the back, check if you're right, and adjust. That is exactly how supervised learning trains a model.

cards.title: What is supervised learning?
cards.body: Supervised learning trains a model on labeled examples — each one paired with the correct answer. The model makes a guess, compares it to the label, and adjusts to get closer next time.

cards.title: A teacher grading every guess
cards.body: Think of a teacher who hands back your homework instantly graded, every single time. Supervised learning works the same way: for every example, the model's guess gets checked against the real answer key, over and over, thousands of times.

cards.title: Labels are everywhere
cards.body: A house-price model trains on past houses labeled with their actual sale price. A photo-tagging model trains on photos labeled "cat" or "dog." An email filter trains on messages already marked spam or not spam. Every one of these has a built-in answer key.

cards.title: The labels don't appear by magic
cards.body: It's easy to assume a model just "knows" the right answers. In reality, a human (or a whole team of humans) had to label every single example ahead of time — that labeling work often takes longer than building the model itself.

cards.title: Guess vs. Answer Key
cards.body: What happens on every single training example:

left.text:
Predicts an answer
e.g. "this is a cat"

right.text:
The real labeled answer
e.g. "actually, a dog"

exercises.prompt: In supervised learning, what does the model compare its guess to?
exercises.explain: Right! Every guess gets checked against the correct label.

exercises.prompt: True or false: In supervised learning, every training example comes with the correct answer already attached.
exercises.explain: True! That correct answer is exactly what makes it "supervised."

exercises.sentence: Supervised learning checks the model's guess against the correct ___ for every example.
exercises.explain: Every example in supervised learning comes with a label — the correct answer to check against.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is an example of supervised learning?

options.text: A spam filter trained on 50,000 emails, each already marked "spam" or "not spam"
A robot that improves at a maze by earning points for reaching the exit
A program that groups news articles into topics nobody named in advance

exercises.explain: Correct! Every email already has its correct label attached, which is the hallmark of supervised learning.

exercises.prompt: A weather app trains a model using ten years of past days, where each day is labeled with the actual high temperature that occurred. The model learns to predict tomorrow's high temperature. Why is this supervised learning?

options.text: Because every training example (a past day) comes with the correct answer (the actual temperature) attached
Because the model receives a reward every time its prediction is close
Because the model has no correct answers to check against
Because weather is too random for any pattern to exist

exercises.explain: Exactly! Each past day is a labeled example with a known correct temperature, so the model can check its guesses against real answers.
